title: Ecclinusa cyanogena
taxonomy: Ecclinusa cyanogena is a species within the plant family Sapotaceae, commonly known as the sapodilla or sapote family. This family consists of approximately 100 genera and over 1,000 species of trees and shrubs. Ecclinusa cyanogena is native to Central and South America, particularly in countries such as Costa Rica and Panama. The Sapotaceae family is often characterized by its diverse array of species with edible fruits, ranging from sapodilla to mamey sapote. The classification of Ecclinusa cyanogena reflects its unique characteristics and its placement within the Sapotaceae family.
uses: Ecclinusa cyanogena is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Research suggests that it may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could be beneficial in treating various health conditions. intro: Ecclesina (Ecclesina or more commonly known as Ecclesina or possibly  more commonly  as  Ecclinusa cyanogena) is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America.
